title: Environmental Impact Statement, Proposed HUD Project Selection Criteria for Subsidized Housing

schema.page_numbers: ESTIMATED from char offsets at 2500 chars/page.
schema.merge_rule: One row per (entity, stance) — stance changes produce separate rows.
schema.cost_note: USD costs are ESTIMATES from settings.PRICES_USD_PER_M; verify against AWS Bedrock invoice.

entries.summary_quote: The criteria have been revised twice as a result of comments received from a large number and wide variety of sources ranging from other HUD elements in Central and Field Offices, public groups such as local housing and redevelopment agencies, individual cities, the U.S. Civil Rights Commission, the Housing Opportunity Council of Metropolitan Washington and innumerable private groups ranging from the National Urban Coalition, the NAACP, League of Women Voters, Americans for Indian Opportunity, and the Citizens Advocate Center, to the National Association of Home Builders, Mortgage Bankers Association of America, and private companies.

mentions.quote: The criteria have been revised twice as a result of comments received from a large number and wide variety of sources ranging from other HUD elements in Central and Field Offices, public groups such as local housing and redevelopment agencies, individual cities, the U.S. Civil Rights Commission, the Housing Opportunity Council of Metropolitan Washington and innumerable private groups ranging from the National Urban Coalition, the NAACP, League of Women Voters, Americans for Indian Opportunity, and the Citizens Advocate Center, to the National Association of Home Builders, Mortgage Bankers Association of America, and private companies.
mentions.stance_basis: listed as having submitted comments that led to revisions of the criteria

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact quote appears verbatim in the 'Alternatives to the Proposed Criteria' section of the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links entity to stated stance
rubric_results.note: Americans for Indian Opportunity is named in the paraphrased list of commenters whose input led to criteria revisions; the narrator sentence establishes this linkage.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: The text only identifies Americans for Indian Opportunity as a commenter/source of comments; no specific position (for or against) is stated, making 'neutral' appropriate.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: 'Americans for Indian Opportunity' is the official organization name and matches the text exactly.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The text confirms they were among the 'innumerable private groups' that provided comments on the proposed criteria.

critic.notes: The summary quote is present verbatim in the cited pages. Americans for Indian Opportunity is listed among commenters whose feedback led to criteria revisions, supporting the 'neutral' stance label and 'commenter on proposed criteria' role. No specific position for or against the criteria is attributed to the organization. [Forced HUMAN_REVIEW: quote not verbatim.]

entries.summary_quote: The criteria have been revised twice as a result of comments received from a large number and wide variety of sources ranging from other HUD elements in Central and Field Offices, public groups such as local housing and redevelopment agencies, individual cities, the U.S. Civil Rights Commission, the Housing Opportunity Council of Metropolitan Washington and innumerable private groups ranging from the National Urban Coalition, the NAACP, League of Women Voters, Americans for Indian Opportunity, and the Citizens Advocate Center, to the National Association of Home Builders, Mortgage Bankers Association of America, and private companies.

mentions.quote: The criteria have been revised twice as a result of comments received from a large number and wide variety of sources ranging from other HUD elements in Central and Field Offices, public groups such as local housing and redevelopment agencies, individual cities, the U.S. Civil Rights Commission, the Housing Opportunity Council of Metropolitan Washington and innumerable private groups ranging from the National Urban Coalition, the NAACP, League of Women Voters, Americans for Indian Opportunity, and the Citizens Advocate Center, to the National Association of Home Builders, Mortgage Bankers Association of America, and private companies.
mentions.stance_basis: listed as having submitted comments that led to revisions of the criteria

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The quote appears verbatim in the 'Alternatives to the Proposed Criteria' section of the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links entity to stated stance
rubric_results.note: Citizens Advocate Center is named in the paraphrased list of commenters whose input led to criteria revisions.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: The text only identifies Citizens Advocate Center as a commenter; no stance (neutral, opposed, in_favor, conditional) is discernible from the passage. However, 'neutral' is arguably the most defensible label for an entity merely listed as a commenter with no stated position.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: Citizens Advocate Center is an organization name and matches the text exactly.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The text confirms they were among the sources that provided comments on the proposed criteria.

critic.notes: The Citizens Advocate Center is listed among a broad range of commenters, but no specific stance is attributed to them — only that they submitted comments. Labeling this 'neutral' is a reasonable inference but not directly supported by the text. A human should determine whether 'neutral' is appropriate or whether this entity should be excluded from stance-coded rows entirely, as the passage conveys no directional position.

entries.entity: Department of Housing and Urban Development
entries.summary_quote: Recommendation That the attached criteria, as revised in the light of comments, be issued as guidance to HUD field offices.

mentions.quote: Recommendation That the attached criteria, as revised in the light of comments, be issued as guidance to HUD field offices.
mentions.stance_basis: recommends issuance of the proposed criteria as revised
mentions.entity_as_written: Department of Housing and Urban Development

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact text 'Recommendation That the attached criteria, as revised in the light of comments, be issued as guidance to HUD field offices.' appears verbatim in the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links entity to stance
rubric_results.note: The document is authored by HUD (the Responsible Agency listed on the Summary Sheet), and the Recommendation section is part of HUD's own Final Environmental Statement, making HUD the implicit author of this recommendation.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: HUD is recommending issuance of its own criteria, clearly indicating support/favor for the proposed action.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: 'Department of Housing and Urban Development' is the official agency name as used in the document.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The Summary Sheet explicitly identifies HUD as the 'Responsible Agency,' supporting the 'responsible agency / proponent' role description.

critic.notes: All checks pass. The quote is verbatim, HUD is clearly the responsible agency and author of the recommendation, and the in_favor stance is unambiguous given that HUD is recommending issuance of its own proposed criteria. The attribution mode of 'paraphrased' is slightly loose since the quote is a direct statement from the document itself, but this does not affect the overall accuracy of the extraction.

entries.summary_quote: Comment: The statement should include a discussion of the potential for misuse of the criteria to place proposed projects in a low priority group to discourage subsidized housing developments in certain areas.

mentions.quote: Comment: The statement should include a discussion of the potential for misuse of the criteria to place proposed projects in a low priority group to discourage subsidized housing developments in certain areas.
mentions.stance_basis: raises concerns about misuse potential and requests procedural/textual improvements without opposing or supporting the proposal overall

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact quote appears verbatim under the 'Department of the Interior' section on the cited pages: 'The statement should include a discussion of the potential for misuse of the criteria to place proposed projects in a low priority group to discourage subsidized housing developments in certain areas.'

rubric_results.check: Attribution links named entity to stated stance
rubric_results.note: The text clearly attributes this comment to 'Department of the Interior' as a commenter on the draft environmental statement.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: The Department of the Interior is raising a concern/criticism about potential misuse of the criteria — this is more accurately 'conditional' or 'opposed' to the criteria as written, not 'neutral'. They are flagging a deficiency in the statement and the criteria.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: 'Department of the Interior' is the official agency name and matches the text exactly.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The Department of the Interior is listed among agencies from which comments were received, and their comments appear in the 'External Comments' section, confirming the role of 'commenting agency'.

critic.notes: The quote is verbatim and the attribution is correct, but the stance label 'neutral' is inaccurate. The Department of the Interior is raising a substantive criticism about a deficiency in the statement and potential for misuse of the criteria, which is better characterized as 'conditional' (supporting the criteria only if the misuse concern is addressed) or possibly 'opposed' to the criteria as written. The stance should be re-labeled as 'conditional' at minimum.

entries.summary_quote: Comment: Item 3 of the questionnaire should be revised to include specific reference to the availability of public transportation which is a critical concern for lower income families.

mentions.quote: Comment: Item 3 of the questionnaire should be revised to include specific reference to the availability of public transportation which is a critical concern for lower income families.
mentions.stance_basis: requests specific revisions to criteria without opposing or endorsing the proposal

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The quote 'Comment: Item 3 of the questionnaire should be revised to include specific reference to the availability of public transportation which is a critical concern for lower income families.' appears verbatim on page 1-10 under the Department of Transportation section.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links entity to stance
rubric_results.note: The text clearly attributes this comment to the Department of Transportation under a heading labeled 'Department of Transportation', with the comment directly following.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: The Department of Transportation is making a specific recommendation/request to revise the questionnaire, which is a 'conditional' stance (suggesting improvement) rather than 'neutral'. This is a substantive comment requesting a change.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: 'Department of Transportation' is the official agency name as used in the document.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The Department of Transportation is listed among agencies from which comments were received, confirming the 'commenting agency' role.

critic.notes: The summary quote is verbatim and the attribution is correct. However, the stance label 'neutral' is incorrect — the Department of Transportation is making a specific recommendation to revise the questionnaire, which constitutes a 'conditional' stance (supporting the proposal with suggested modifications). The stance should be re-labeled as 'conditional'. [Forced HUMAN_REVIEW: quote not verbatim.]

entries.summary_quote: Comment: Summarize in the environmental impact statement the other specific environmental requirements which must be met by project applications.

mentions.quote: Comment: Summarize in the environmental impact statement the other specific environmental requirements which must be met by project applications.
mentions.stance_basis: requests expansions and clarifications to criteria without opposing or endorsing the proposal

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact text 'Summarize in the environmental impact statement the other specific environmental requirements which must be met by project applications.' appears verbatim on page 1-10 under the EPA section.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links EPA to the stated stance
rubric_results.note: The cited text clearly attributes this comment to 'Environmental Protection Agency' as a commenter, with the quote appearing directly under that heading.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: The EPA is making a substantive recommendation/request to expand the EIS content, which is more accurately 'conditional' (requesting improvement) rather than 'neutral'. A neutral stance would imply no position taken.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: 'Environmental Protection Agency' is the correct official name for this federal agency.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: EPA appears in the list of agencies from which comments were received, and their comments are presented in the External Comments section, confirming 'commenting agency' role.

critic.notes: The quote is verbatim and the attribution is correct, but the stance label 'neutral' is inaccurate. The EPA is making specific recommendations for improvement to the EIS and criteria, which constitutes a 'conditional' stance — supporting the proposal but requesting modifications. The stance should be re-labeled as 'conditional' to accurately reflect the EPA's position.
